title: sophora alopecuroides var. tomentosa
intro: Sophora alopecuroides var. tomentosa, also known as the hairy sophora or hairy kudzu, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub native to China, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The plant's roots and stems are rich in isoflavones, which are believed to possess medicinal properties that can help to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sophora alopecuroides var. tomentosa is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in herbal capsules or tablets to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
